chief_complaint: A 32-year-old healthy Lebanese female patient presented with acute coronary syndrome.
history: The patient had no significant past medical history or cardiovascular risk factors. She developed acute coronary syndrome 8 months after initiating oral contraceptive pills (Yasmin tablets; 3.0 mg drospirenone and 0.030 mg ethinyl estradiol).
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided in the case report.
labs: ECG showed ST elevation myocardial infarction. Blood tests and imaging results are not provided in the case report.
correct_diagnosis: Massive left main coronary artery thrombosis induced by oral contraceptive pills
differentials: Massive left main coronary artery thrombosis induced by oral contraceptive pills
Acute coronary syndrome due to atherosclerotic coronary artery disease
Acute coronary syndrome due to aortic dissection
Acute coronary syndrome due to myocardial bridge
Acute coronary syndrome due to coronary artery vasculitis
correct_ranking: Massive left main coronary artery thrombosis induced by oral contraceptive pills
Acute coronary syndrome due to atherosclerotic coronary artery disease
Acute coronary syndrome due to aortic dissection
Acute coronary syndrome due to myocardial bridge
